sector: Electrical Equipment — custom-engineered MV/LV switchgear, integrated power-control rooms, bus duct & behind-the-meter power distribution for data centers, electric utility, LNG/oil & gas, petrochemical and traction.
bull_thesis: POWL is one of the cleanest small/mid-cap pure-plays on the electrical-distribution bottleneck of the AI build-out, and the evidence is in orders, not narrative. Q2 FY26 (qtr ended 3/31/26) printed orders of $490M (+97% YoY), a 1.7x book-to-bill, and backlog of $1.8B (+33% YoY, +12% QoQ) with visibility 'well into fiscal 2028.' Post-quarter (5/4/26) Powell won a >$400M greenfield data center award — the largest in company history — for behind-the-meter / outside-the-box power infrastructure (on-site generation, power islands), with future internal phases under pursuit and a ~2-year burn through FY2028. The strategic upgrade is that Powell is migrating up the value chain from 'utility connect' to behind-the-meter power islands precisely as interconnect queues stall, expanding its TAM ('the TAM on behind-the-meter is going up… beyond a straight utility connect'). Demand is broadening, not narrowing: in the same quarter Commercial & Other Industrial grew +35% and Electric Utility +14%, more than offsetting a −37% petrochemical decline — the 'reduced cyclicality' thesis being earned in real time. The balance sheet is a fortress: ~$545M cash & ST investments, ZERO debt, $51M quarterly operating cash flow, equity $709M, and ROIC ~35% (FY25). Copper is hedged to lock backlog margins. Optionality the market isn't paying for: a new defense/government funnel (REMSDAQ + DPA §303 designating electrical products defense-essential) and a multi-year LNG-export build-out. FY25 already delivered the step-up the prior narrative implied (rev $1.0B→$1.1B, GM 27.0%→29.4%, EPS +12% to $4.22). If Powell sanctions the $70–100M greenfield (+$100–250M revenue capacity) and converts even half the backlog inflection, FY27–28 EPS can compound double-digits off a high-ROIC, net-cash base — and the multiple can hold because the business is structurally less cyclical than the market remembers. Conditions: greenfield sanctioned and built on time; skilled-labor/floor-space constraint solved; DC award executes without phase delay; AI-capex / LNG capex cycle stays favorable through FY2028.
bear_thesis: The demand is real but the price has already paid for it. POWL trades ~$299 (~$10.8B cap) at ~28–30x forward EPS and ~17–25x EV/EBITDA after a vertical re-rate (52-wk range $56.70→$328; +10% on the Q2 print, near all-time highs), and the marquee catalyst — the >$400M DC award — is POST-QUARTER and FULLY PUBLIC. The re-rate it triggered has largely FIRED; you are buying after the news. The fundamentals flash a warning the headline hides: despite +6% revenue, EPS FELL ~1% YoY ($1.27→$1.25) as higher comp, R&D (1.4% of rev) and SG&A (+$4M, REMSDAQ) ate the growth — the orders are NOT yet converting to the bottom line, and the multiple assumes they will, fast. Conversion is gated by a hard physical ceiling management openly admits: floor space + skilled labor. The $70–100M greenfield that the FY27–28 capacity bridge depends on is UNSANCTIONED ('a decision within the next few quarters'); rapid $8M fab spend and 50k-sq-ft leases are stopgaps. Margin is near a cyclical high and flat-to-down YoY (29.6%, −30bp), with ~90bp of the QoQ gain from one-time project closeouts — not run-rate. The moat is NARROW (engineering/qualification, not patent), and management itself flags intensifying competition: 'a lot of new entrants, some new private equity money coming in' — capacity will chase this demand and compress returns. The marquee award is single-project, single-undisclosed-counterparty concentration; a phase delay or cancel is a real tail. And the entire thesis rides the SAME shared macro factor — AI-capex durability — as the rest of Basket B; an AI-capex guide-down de-grosses the whole cohort at once. At 30x, any conversion stumble, capacity miss, or capex-cycle wobble re-rates this hard. Conditions that confirm: an EPS quarter where backlog still doesn't drop through; greenfield delayed/over-budget; an AI-capex scare; or competitive pricing showing up in book-to-bill.
sop_bull: $330–360/sh. Whole-company intrinsic (the business is reported by end-market, not as separable segments warranting a true SoP). Core operating business: apply a premium-but-justified ~30x to a forward EPS power of ~$6.0–6.5 (FY27 normalized — backlog conversion + DC award burn + LNG/utility ramp on a 1.7x book-to-bill) = ~$180–195/sh on EPS×multiple, OR ~16–18x an EV/EBITDA on ~$280–300M forward EBITDA on the $1.8B+ backlog ≈ ~$290–315/sh enterprise. Add net cash overlay: ~$545M (~$15/sh) with zero debt. Add unpriced optionality: defense/gov't funnel + LNG multi-year + greenfield capacity (+$100–250M revenue) credited at ~$20–35/sh. Cohort cross-check: POWL at ~30x sits below GEV (~71x fwd) and NVT (~38x fwd) and roughly in line with ETN (~32x) / HUBB (~29x) despite the cleanest book-to-bill — supports the upper-bound multiple. Bull SoP ≈ $330–360/sh.
sop_bear: $180–215/sh. Normalize the multiple toward the cohort floor and the earnings toward run-rate. Apply a de-rated ~22–24x (a switchgear contractor with a NARROW moat, cyclical end-markets, and flat-to-down YoY EPS does not deserve a secular-compounder multiple) to a more conservative ~$5.0–5.5 normalized EPS (backlog converts slower; one-time closeouts and pricing tailwinds fade; comp/R&D/SG&A keep eating growth) = ~$110–130/sh on earnings, OR ~12–13x EV/EBITDA on ~$240–260M EBITDA ≈ ~$150–175/sh enterprise. Add net cash ~$15/sh. Haircut the DC-award optionality (single undisclosed counterparty, phase risk) to near zero. Bear SoP ≈ $180–215/sh — i.e., even the bear isn't catastrophic because of the net-cash, high-ROIC base; it is a de-rating from a stretched multiple, not a value-trap impairment.
sop_breakdown: Base-case whole-company intrinsic ~$255/sh (~25x a blended forward EPS power of ~$5.6–6.0 = ~$140–170 on earnings; cross-checked at ~14–15x EV/EBITDA on ~$265M forward EBITDA ≈ ~$215–235 enterprise; + ~$15/sh net cash; + ~$15–20/sh credited but haircut optionality for defense/LNG/greenfield). Reconciles bull $330–360 and bear $180–215 toward the midpoint, deliberately tilted DOWN from the bull because the load-bearing DC catalyst has FIRED, the multiple is near cohort-high, and EPS conversion is still unproven (EPS −1% YoY on +6% revenue). At ~$299 the stock is ~17% ABOVE this base-case fair value — the AI-scale-out optionality is largely priced in.
risk_reward: Unfavorable-to-balanced from $299. Downside-to-break (bear SoP ~$180–215, mid ~$200) ≈ −33%; upside-to-fair-value (base $255) ≈ −15% (i.e., the stock sits ABOVE base-case fair value); upside-to-bull ($330–360, mid ~$345) ≈ +15%. Roughly 1:0.5 reward:risk to the base case — you are paying up for a fired catalyst. Skew only turns attractive on a meaningful pullback (toward ~$220–240) or a fresh, hard-dated catalyst (greenfield sanction + a second hyperscaler award).
catalyst_status: FIRED. The load-bearing catalyst — the >$400M greenfield data center award, 'largest in company history' — was announced 5/4/26 (post-Q2), is fully public, and drove a ~+10% move into a stock near all-time highs ($328 52-wk high vs $299 now). The order/backlog inflection (orders +97%, book-to-bill 1.7x, backlog $1.8B) is also already reported and in the price. The DC award is a single greenfield first-phase with an undisclosed counterparty and a ~2-year burn — already-printed, not a forward asymmetric event. The only PENDING_HARD items are second-order: the $70–100M greenfield-capacity sanction ('within the next few quarters') and any FOLLOW-ON internal-phase / second-hyperscaler award — neither yet dated or binding. Net status: FIRED on the primary catalyst; the re-rate is largely spent.
peer_comps_note: TRUE peers = the data-center / grid electrical-distribution cohort (scale-out Basket B GRID layer): GE Vernova (GEV, grid + on-site power, ~71x fwd P/E, the cohort's richest), nVent (NVT, switchgear + DC enclosures/power, ~38x fwd P/E, +26–28% '26 sales guide on DC demand), Eaton (ETN, electrical/DC power, ~32x fwd P/E), Hubbell (HUBB, T&D + utility, ~28–29x P/E); secondary mega-cap references Schneider, ABB, Vertiv, Mitsubishi Electric. WHERE POWL RANKS (multiples verified live, June 2026): VALUATION — mid-cohort at ~28–30x fwd P/E / ~17–25x EV/EBITDA; cheaper than GEV/NVT, in line with ETN/HUBB, but the SMALLEST and most project-/end-market-concentrated, so it should arguably trade at a slight discount to the diversified mega-caps, not in line. GROWTH — best-in-cohort on the leading indicator (orders +97%, book-to-bill 1.7x, backlog +33%) but only +6% on reported revenue (conversion lag); NVT's +26–28% guided revenue growth is currently higher on the P&L. MARGINS — GM ~29.6% is solid for an EPC-flavored switchgear maker but BELOW the diversified-product peers (ETN/HUBB run higher operating margins); POWL is more project/contract-margin in character. RETURNS — ROIC ~35% (FY25) is excellent and at/above the cohort, flattered by the cyclical wave. BALANCE SHEET — POWL is the cleanest: zero debt + ~$545M net cash vs leveraged peers (ETN/NVT/HUBB carry net debt). AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE — high and direct (behind-the-meter DC power), but more concentrated/binary than the diversified peers — higher beta to the single AI-capex factor. Net: POWL is the high-quality, net-cash, best-book-to-bill name in the cohort, trading at a fair-to-full multiple that does NOT obviously discount its smaller scale and single-factor concentration.
role_in_scaleout: Powell is a behind-the-meter / outside-the-box electrical-distribution supplier — custom MV/LV switchgear, integrated power-control rooms, bus duct and on-site/power-island power infrastructure that sits between the utility (or on-site generation) and the data hall; a hard physical chokepoint every hyperscaler/sovereign-AI capex dollar must pass through to energize a campus. Demand durability is HIGH near-term (FY2028 backlog visibility, 1.7x book-to-bill, LNG + utility + DC legs) but CYCLICAL, not annuity: it is gated by the AI-capex / electrification capex cycle and by Powell's own capacity to build, with new entrants and PE money signaling competitive supply response over time.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT meaningfully offside — and that is the problem. After the +97% order print and the >$400M DC award, sell-side and the tape have fully embraced the 'grid bottleneck / reduced-cyclicality / AI-power compounder' narrative; the stock 5x'd off its 52-wk low ($56.70→$299) and trades ~28–30x forward. The expectations-arbitrage is therefore THIN-to-NEGATIVE: the market is extrapolating the 1.7x book-to-bill into a smooth FY27–28 earnings ramp and granting POWL a near-secular-compounder multiple in line with diversified mega-caps (ETN ~32x, HUBB ~29x) despite POWL being smaller, more project-concentrated, and more single-factor (AI-capex) exposed. The genuinely under-appreciated facts cut BOTH ways: bullishly, the defense/gov't (DPA §303) and LNG legs and the behind-the-meter TAM expansion are not fully in numbers; bearishly, the street is under-weighting that EPS FELL 1% YoY on +6% revenue (conversion lag), that ~90bp of the margin beat was one-time closeouts, and that the greenfield capacity needed to convert backlog is UNSANCTIONED. The incorrect consensus assumption is 'orders = near-term EPS' — Powell just demonstrated the opposite. With the catalyst fired and the multiple full, there is no obvious mispriced delta to harvest at $299.
valley_of_death: POWL has NO balance-sheet valley of death — zero debt, ~$545M net cash, $51M quarterly OCF, no maturity wall, no cash-burn hump. The near-term (3–9 month) risk is to the STOCK, not the solvency: (1) Conversion-lag prints — the next 1–2 quarters can again show revenue/EPS lagging the backlog (the +97% orders won't hit the P&L for several quarters given the ~2-year DC burn), and at 30x any in-line-but-not-accelerating EPS quarter can de-rate the stock 15–25%. (2) Capacity overhang — the unsanctioned $70–100M greenfield: a delay, an over-budget sanction, or visible skilled-labor constraints would directly challenge the FY27–28 bridge. (3) Margin normalization — losing the ~90bp one-time closeout tailwind and any pricing give-back as new entrants compete. (4) The shared-factor tail — an AI-capex guide-down from a hyperscaler (the regime doc flags AI-capex as ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant two-sided risk) would de-gross the ENTIRE GRID cohort simultaneously; POWL's high single-factor beta means it falls hardest. (5) The Q1-FY26 'Iran/oil' precedent (VIX 0→26 in days) shows the tape's tail is live. None of these impair intrinsic value, but all can punish a 30x stock before the FY2028 backlog converts.
positioning_washout: No mechanical-selling setup and no forced washout to wait for — but also no capitulated base to buy. POWL pays a token dividend ($0.27/sh, $6.5M/6mo) so there is no yield-focused holder base to flush on a cut, and no pivot that forces liquidation. The 3-for-1 split (4/6/26) + authorized-share increase to 90M improved liquidity and likely BROADENED the holder base (retail/momentum) rather than concentrating it — which cuts the other way: a momentum/growth holder base near all-time highs is PRONE to mechanical de-grossing on any AI-capex scare or growth-factor unwind, not to forced buying. The name screens as a momentum/quality-growth holding, not a special-sit with a positioning vacuum to exploit. The washout to wait for, if any, is a broad AI-capex / growth-factor de-gross that takes POWL back toward its base-case fair value (~$255) or below — that is the entry, not now.
forcing_function: No single hard-dated re-rating event is pending in the near window — the primary forcing event (the >$400M DC award) already FIRED on 5/4/26. The forward dated items are softer and second-order: (1) Q3 FY26 earnings (~early Aug 2026) — the first read on whether backlog is starting to convert to revenue/EPS and whether margins hold ex-closeouts; this is the next 'shatter-or-confirm' print. (2) The greenfield-capacity sanction decision, management-guided 'within the next few quarters' — a sanction (with a second hyperscaler award attached) would be a genuine new catalyst; a deferral would pressure the multiple. (3) Any FOLLOW-ON internal-phase or second data-center award — un-dated but the real bull forcing function. (4) FY2026 results (Nov 2026) for the full conversion picture. None is a binding/dated asymmetric event today, which is exactly why the name is a watchlist, not an aggressive entry.
moderator_conclusion: POWL is a genuinely high-quality business at a full-to-stretched price with its load-bearing catalyst already fired — the textbook 'great company, late entry' profile, and the regime makes that worse, not better. Applying CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME (2026-06-05): the regime explicitly PENALIZES already-fired / already-ran catalysts and AI-capex-single-factor concentration, and flags AI-capex as the dominant shared macro driver under the tape (~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth) demanding resolution-driver independence at the book level. POWL fails on both counts — its >$400M DC award has FIRED (re-rate spent, stock +10% on the print, near all-time highs after a 5x off the lows), and its ENTIRE thesis rides the same AI-capex factor as the rest of Basket B (GRID), the opposite of independence. The forensic quality is real and corroborated (book-to-bill 1.7x, backlog $1.8B +33% with FY2028 visibility, ROIC ~35%, zero debt, ~$545M net cash, credibility 4/STRENGTHENING), but at ~$299 the stock trades ~17% ABOVE base-case SoP fair value (~$255) and at ~28–30x forward — in line with diversified mega-cap peers despite being smaller and more concentrated. EPS actually FELL 1% YoY on +6% revenue: the orders are NOT yet converting, and the multiple already prices the conversion as done. Risk/reward from here is ~1:0.5 to the base case (−15% to fair value, +15% to bull, −33% to bear). VERDICT C / PASS at this price, conviction 3. This is NOT a value-trap pass — the moat is narrow-but-stable, the secular setup is a TAILWIND (electrification/AI-power), and the balance sheet is pristine — so value_conviction is also 3, not lower: stripped of the catalyst/regime overlay, the business is high-quality but trades modestly above intrinsic fair value, so the pure-value case is fair, not compelling. Re-engage on a meaningful pullback toward ~$220–240 (a broad AI-capex / growth-factor de-gross is the most likely trigger), or on a NEW hard catalyst — the greenfield sanction paired with a second hyperscaler award. Watchlist, not buy.
interrogator_dossier:
# Powell Industries (POWL) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator pass · scale-out Basket B (GRID). Latest data: Q2 FY2026 (qtr ended 3/31/26), reported 5/4/26; full-year FY2025 (ended 9/30/25). Price ~$299 (post 3-for-1 split eff. 4/6/26), market cap ~$10.8B.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
Powell has cleanly metamorphosed from a cyclical oil-&-gas/petrochemical-levered switchgear contractor into one of the cleanest small/mid-cap pure-plays on the electrical-distribution bottleneck of the AI build-out. The pivot is evidence-backed: FY24→FY25 stepped revenue $1.0B→$1.1B, GM 27.0%→29.4%, EPS $3.77→$4.22 on LNG/DC/utility demand. By Q2 FY26 the story re-anchored on data centers + behind-the-meter power, marked by the >$400M greenfield DC award (5/4/26) — 'the largest project award in company history' — plus new defense/gov't (REMSDAQ + DPA §303) and LNG legs. Arc = EXPANSION; no drivers dropped.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS
Revenue +6% to $296.6M MATCHES but lags orders +97% — backlog is the story, not the P&L. Demand broad (Commercial +35%, Utility +14%, O&G +11%) but Petrochemical −37% shows legacy volatility. Orders $490M, book-to-bill 1.7x, backlog $1.8B (+33%) into FY2028 — STRONGLY supported, the load-bearing fact. GM 29.6% (−30bp YoY, +120bp QoQ of which ~90bp one-time closeouts) — honest, not hyped, near a cyclical high. TENSION: EPS FELL ~1% ($1.27→$1.25) despite +6% revenue (comp + R&D 1.4% + SG&A drag) — growth not yet converting. Fortress balance sheet ($545M cash, zero debt, $51M OCF, ROIC ~35%) fully supported. Capacity (greenfield $70–100M unsanctioned) is the gating risk. Net: narrative richer than P&L, poorer than backlog — in transit.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE
RISING — confident but disciplined, not promotional. Offensive on TAM ('behind-the-meter TAM is going up… beyond a straight utility connect', 'largest award in history', LNG 'multiyear build-out') yet governed by execution realism ('two-year burn', capacity = floor space + talent, 'we engage them on sequencing'). Candid on competition ('much more competitive… new entrants, some new private equity money').

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY
Medium-High. Powell gives light formal guidance (capex, R&D %), so less to miss; what it commits lands (FY25 margin/EPS step-up delivered; capex $12–13M specific; R&D 1.4% in the 1–1.5% band). Watch: no revenue-conversion guide on the $1.8B backlog beyond 'visibility into FY2028.'

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS
Backlog conversion / capacity ('can you build it?' — honest but unresolved, greenfield unsanctioned); margin durability (mgmt volunteered ~90bp was one-time closeouts); DC concentration / undisclosed counterparty (DC = low-twenties of the 29% commercial backlog); pricing (narrowed to 'supply-constrained product areas', no broad pricing-power claim).

## 6. RED / GREEN FLAGS
🟢 Backlog $1.8B (+33%), book-to-bill 1.7x, FY2028 visibility. 🟢 Net cash ~$545M, zero debt, ROIC ~35%, $51M OCF. 🟢 >$400M behind-the-meter DC award = TAM expansion. 🟢 Real diversification (Commercial +35% vs Petrochem −37%). 🟢 Defense/LNG optionality added. 🔴 EPS FELL ~1% YoY on +6% revenue. 🔴 Capacity is the hard ceiling; greenfield unsanctioned. 🔴 Competition intensifying (new entrants, PE money). 🔴 Marquee catalyst FIRED (post-quarter, public, +10% on print). 🔴 Single-project, undisclosed-counterparty concentration.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS
Lexicon shift to 'behind-the-meter'/'power island' (moving up the value chain as interconnect queues stall). 'Reduced cyclicality' repeated as a thesis — mgmt re-rating its own multiple narrative. Defense/gov't recurring-revenue funnel being seeded. Copper hedged 'as insurance to protect backlog margins' — locks conversion economics. 3-for-1 split + 90M authorized = confidence/liquidity + capacity/M&A flexibility.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT
Trajectory STRENGTHENING. Powell turned a cyclical switchgear business into a genuine grid/behind-the-meter AI-power beneficiary (1.7x book-to-bill, +33% backlog to FY2028, record >$400M award, ROIC ~35%, zero-debt/$545M net cash). Single most important thing to weigh: this is a backlog-conversion/capacity story whose marquee catalyst has already FIRED into a stock near all-time highs at ~28–30x forward — demand is real and durable, but how much is already paid for, and can Powell physically build it (unsanctioned greenfield)? Moat NARROW, STABLE: intangible/qualification-based (custom safety-critical MV switchgear, long qualification cycles, spec switching costs) — real but not patent/network/scale; ROIC high-and-rising (a widening tell) BUT GM flat-to-down YoY and competition intensifying cap it at NARROW/STABLE, returns cyclically elevated and unproven through a downturn. Secular threat MANAGEABLE and largely a TAILWIND (picks-and-shovels to electrification/AI-power, no displacement/disintermediation risk); genuine risks are cyclicality (AI-capex/LNG/petrochemical capex cycle) and competitive supply response — cyclical/competitive, not terminal. Cheapness question is 'is the AI-capex optionality already priced,' not a value-trap.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found that breaks the bear/PASS thesis — every load-bearing claim verifies against the primary 8-K press release. The bear case (catalyst FIRED, demand priced in, EPS not converting) is independently confirmed: Q2 FY26 EPS FELL to $1.25 from $1.27 prior on +6% revenue ($296.6M); the >$400M data center award is POST-QUARTER (announced 5/4/26) and fully public, and the re-rate has already fired (split-adjusted 52-wk high $328.00 on 5/11/26, now ~$293). The PASS verdict survives the kill-check.
skeptic_corrections: Minor: sop_fair_value $255 and the bear EPS/margin claims are built on the latest reported quarter (Q2 FY26, ended 3/31/26, reported 5/4/26) — NOT a stale anchor. Current price in the debate ($299.14) is modestly above the mid-June tape (~$292.70 per multiple quote sources), so the 'stock ~17% above fair value' gap is slightly overstated (closer to ~+15%) and the 'near all-time highs' framing should read 'down ~11% from the split-adjusted 52-wk high $328' — the re-rate is largely spent but the name has come off the high. The $70-100M greenfield capacity figure is from the earnings-call commentary (CFO 'prudently invest in incremental capacity' + Seeking Alpha call coverage), not the press-release body — correctly flagged in the dossier as UNSANCTIONED.
skeptic_evidence: Powell Industries Q2 FY2026 press release (GlobeNewswire/IR, 5/4/26): revenue $296.6M +6%, net income $45.9M, diluted EPS $1.25 vs $1.27 prior-year (EPS DOWN on revenue UP), gross margin 29.6%, new orders $490M +97%, book-to-bill 1.7x, backlog $1.8B +33% YoY; cash & ST investments ~$545M, no debt, stockholders' equity $709.1M; 3-for-1 split effective 4/2/26.

Powell 8-K / press release (5/4/26): 'Subsequent to the end of the second quarter, the Company was awarded an additional mega data center order with a value exceeding $400 million related to a behind-the-meter design of on-site generation assets' — the largest order in company history; POST-QUARTER and public, confirming catalyst FIRED.

Seeking Alpha (5/4/26): 'Powell outlines $70M-$100M capacity option as it lands $400M+ data center mega order' — confirms the greenfield capacity expansion is an UNSANCTIONED option ('a decision within the next few quarters'), not a committed build; first-phase award has a 2-2.5 year burn.

Quote sources (mid-June 2026): POWL ~$292.70; split-adjusted 52-wk range $56.70-$328.00, all-time/52-wk high $328.00 reached 5/11/26 — re-rate fired post-award; stock now ~11% below the high, ~5x off the $56.70 low.

scale.role: Switchgear / electrical distribution for data centers + energy; >$400M record DC award, orders +97%, B2B 1.7x.
scale.rationale: Strong levered franchise with a fresh record >$400M behind-the-meter DC award (largest in company history) and orders +97%, but smaller and more cyclical (legacy oil/gas mix) than the grid CORE — a solid LEVER. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED (every claim verified; catalyst FIRED, EPS actually fell to $1.25 on +6% revenue), so NO haircut — conviction reads at the full ~52 franchise. Re-rate largely spent (~11% off the split-adjusted high); wait.
scale.demand_durability: Medium-high — DC switchgear is bolting on fast, but legacy oil/gas/industrial mix dilutes the pure-AI durability.
